skill.name: senior-fullstack
skill.description: A senior fullstack development guide for planning, scaffolding, and reviewing React, Next.js, Node.js, GraphQL, and PostgreSQL projects.
skill.summary: Plan, scaffold, and review modern fullstack web applications.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found no malicious intent, prompt injection, network access, dynamic execution, or credential exfiltration. Most external command, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and environment-file alerts are Markdown examples or generic prose. The only retained concern is low-risk filesystem output because helper scripts can write JSON reports to a user-selected path.

low_findings.title: User-Selected Report Output Path
low_findings.description: The helper scripts write JSON results to the path supplied through the output option. This is expected CLI behavior, but users should choose paths carefully to avoid overwriting local files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file write is direct and present in all three scripts. Risk remains low because the path is explicitly provided by the user and no network or secret access is present.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The reported command locations are Markdown usage examples and common development commands. I found no shell execution API, command construction, or automatic command runner in the skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are fenced Markdown code blocks or documentation references. The Python scripts do not call subprocess, os.system, eval, or similar execution APIs.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Prose Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts point to descriptive text such as the frontmatter description and reference headings. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant files contain generic Markdown prose and TypeScript examples only. There is no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or custom crypto implementation in the reviewed content.

low_findings.title: Environment File Alert Is Setup Documentation
low_findings.description: The environment-file alert is a setup example that copies an example file to a local environment file. It does not read, print, transmit, or request existing secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited command is a standard local setup instruction in Markdown. No script accesses .env content and no network code exists to exfiltrate values.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are Generic Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts point to generic documentation headings and troubleshooting instructions. No commands collect host, user, network, or process information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is ordinary reference material and does not include discovery commands. No evidence found for host reconnaissance or data collection behavior.

content.user_title: Build Fullstack Apps with Senior Guidance
content.value_statement: Teams need consistent architecture, setup, and review practices for modern web applications. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through fullstack planning, scaffolding, and quality review workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guides project setup for React, Next.js, Node.js, GraphQL, and PostgreSQL applications.
Documents repeatable development workflows for setup, quality checks, implementation, and deployment commands.
Provides local Python helper scripts for scaffold-style analysis and JSON report output.
Summarizes architecture, technology stack, workflow, performance, and security best practices.
Supports code review planning through documented quality, maintainability, and troubleshooting guidance.
limitations: The included Python scripts are lightweight templates and do not generate complete application code.
Reference documents contain generic examples, not framework-specific production implementations.
Deployment commands require user review before use in a real infrastructure environment.
The skill does not replace project-specific security, accessibility, or performance testing.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Fullstack Product
use_cases.description: Define the stack, project structure, workflow, and quality gates before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Review an Existing Web App
use_cases.description: Use the checklists and analyzer template to identify maintainability, testing, and workflow gaps.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Development Practices
use_cases.description: Create shared guidance for setup, common commands, architecture patterns, and deployment preparation.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Practical Stack
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the senior-fullstack skill to recommend a simple stack for a new SaaS dashboard. Include frontend, backend, database, testing, and deployment choices.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Project Setup Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the senior-fullstack workflow guidance to create a setup checklist for a Next.js, Node.js, GraphQL, and PostgreSQL application.

prompt_templates.title: Review Architecture Decisions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the senior-fullstack references to review my proposed architecture. Focus on maintainability, data flow, performance risks, and security controls.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Development Workflow Maturity
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the senior-fullstack skill to assess our repository workflow. Evaluate setup, testing, linting, CI, deployment readiness, observability, and documentation gaps.

output_examples.input: Plan a web app stack for a small business CRM.
output: A recommended stack with React or Next.js, Node.js services, PostgreSQL storage, and a clear deployment path.
A short rationale for each choice, including complexity, team fit, and expected maintenance cost.
A first-week implementation checklist covering setup, data modeling, testing, and review points.

output_examples.input: Review our project workflow before launch.
output: A launch-readiness review covering tests, linting, environment setup, deployment commands, and operational risks.
Prioritized recommendations grouped by immediate fixes, near-term improvements, and longer-term engineering investments.

output_examples.input: Improve architecture documentation for a GraphQL application.
output: A documentation outline for service boundaries, schema ownership, data access, security controls, and troubleshooting procedures.
A concise list of missing decisions that should be resolved before the next implementation phase.

best_practices: Review generated plans against the actual repository, team skills, and production constraints before implementation.
Run helper scripts only on intended project paths and keep output files inside the workspace.
Treat deployment and infrastructure commands as examples that require project-specific validation.
anti_patterns: Do not run deployment commands from documentation without checking credentials, context, and target environment.
Do not assume the generic reference files cover all security or compliance needs for a regulated project.
Do not use the scaffold scripts as a substitute for real tests, static analysis, or dependency scanning.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps plan, scaffold, and review modern fullstack web application work using documented senior engineering practices.

faq.question: Does it generate a complete production application?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance, templates, and lightweight helper scripts, but implementation still requires project-specific work.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no malicious behavior, but notes low-risk local file output in the helper scripts.

faq.question: Why were high-risk static findings downgraded?
faq.answer: The cited high-risk patterns were Markdown examples or generic prose, not executable malware behavior.

faq.question: What should users check before running commands?
faq.answer: Users should confirm paths, environment files, deployment targets, and credentials before running any documented command.
